_notes.design: 6 fresh candidates (72h window); 3 dropped by URL dedup. Good category spread: tokens ×2, figma ×2, render ×1, workflow ×1. Outlets: Smashing Magazine, UX Collective ×2, Engadget, Builder.io, VentureBeat. No major content clusters. Smashing Magazine accessibility item has an indirect AI hook (AI-accelerated UI generation context) — curator may treat as borderline.
_notes.music: 8 fresh candidates (336h window); 0 dropped by dedup. TIDAL monetization cluster: items 1, 2, 7 (Hypebot, DJ Mag, Engadget) all cover the same TIDAL AI royalty policy — curator should pick at most 1. DJ Mag ×3 (collection cap hit). Other items are distinct: Traxsource AI labeling, 21M songs watchdog tool, Waveform Free 14 AI assistant, Yamaha Creator Pass subscription, YouTube AI training consent concerns.
_notes.movies: 11 fresh candidates (336h window); 4 dropped by dedup. Adobe/Topaz Labs acquisition cluster: items 6, 7, 10 (RedShark News, No Film School, postPerspective) all cover the same story — curator should pick 1. Variety ×2, IndieWire ×2, postPerspective ×3 (collection cap). Strong original stories include Guadagnino 'Artificial' film (Neon), AI dubbing microdrama partnership, Pixar-alumni AI short, and the 'AI as Hollywood's cosmetic surgery' investigation.
_notes.games: 9 fresh candidates (336h window); 0 dropped by dedup. UE6/AI developer-reaction cluster dominates: items 1, 3, 5, 8, 9 all touch UE6 AI integration fallout — curator should pick 1–2. David Gaider 'plague' cluster: items 4 and 6 (GamesRadar, PC Gamer) both quote him on AI being unfit — pick 1. Wccftech ×3 (collection cap). Strongest distinct angles: CDPR on fully AI-made games incoming, PlayStation swaps 'PC' for 'AI', and Sjoerd de Jong leaving Epic.
_notes.books: 7 fresh candidates (336h window); 0 dropped by dedup. Beijing International Book Fair cluster: items 1, 2, 7 (The Bookseller, Publishers Weekly ×2) all cover BIBF 2026 AI publishing — curator should pick 1. Publishers Weekly ×3 (collection cap). Strongest distinct items: Publishing Perspectives on Bookpact.ai + ElevenLabs/Odyssey audiobook, The Creative Penn on AI narration voice licensing debate, The Bookseller on AI and neurodiverse authorship.

design.title: Why Accessibility Is An Operational Capability, Not A Feature
design.excerpt: Teams can generate UI faster than ever, but they still have to guarantee that what they ship is usable, secure, and maintainable. Accessibility is not a feature—it is an operational capability that must be built in from the start, not audited at the end, especially as AI accelerates UI generation.

design.title: What are hypertokens? The layer between tokens and components, rebuilt for agents
design.excerpt: A hypertoken is a named bundle of style properties—coined by Jake Albaugh at Figma's Config 2026—that sits one level above design tokens and generates the CSS class, Figma text style, and Swift struct for iOS from a single definition, making design systems agent-readable.

design.title: Google expands personalized intelligence to Gemini app image creation
design.excerpt: Google is letting Gemini reference a user's Gmail and Google Photos to generate personalised images—showing yourself in a scene or adding a family member to a template. Personalized intelligence rolls out to all eligible US users, broadening AI-assisted creative image generation.

design.title: Figma just made your design system debt everyone's problem. Now use it.
design.excerpt: Figma's Check designs tool compares a file against your design system and flags hard-coded values, contrast failures, and detached components. Design system drift is now a visible number on screen that everyone in a review can see—changing who feels accountable when the system is inconsistent.

design.title: Introducing /visual-plan: Scannable Claude Code plans
design.excerpt: Builder.io's /visual-plan skill turns Claude Code's agent output into a scannable, structured plan that designers can review and approve before execution, and /visual-recap generates a visual summary of what the agent actually changed—bridging agentic coding and design-team oversight.

design.title: Google's Gemini Omni Flash hits the API, turning enterprise video production into a conversation
design.excerpt: Gemini Omni Flash is now available via API, letting teams generate and edit 720p video through natural language conversation—a finished clip can be revised with a follow-up prompt at $0.10 per generated second, making internal explainer and training videos economically viable for creative teams.

music.title: TIDAL Moves Streaming's AI Debate From Ethics to Economics
music.excerpt: TIDAL announced that tracks determined to be primarily AI-generated will no longer be eligible to receive royalties or participate in its direct-to-fan monetization programs, effective July 15. Rather than a blanket ban, the platform draws a line between AI-assisted human creativity (still eligible) and fully AI-generated output (demonetized), shifting the industry debate from ethics to economics.

music.title: TIDAL excludes AI-generated music from royalty payments in new policy update
music.excerpt: TIDAL has updated its payment policy to exclude AI-generated music from royalty pools, with the policy taking effect on July 15. The platform will require distributors to flag AI-generated content on upload, and will remove monetization rights from tracks identified as primarily AI-generated rather than AI-assisted.

music.title: Traxsource to label music as human-made or AI-assisted across platform
music.excerpt: Electronic music download store Traxsource announced it will introduce a labeling system to distinguish human-made tracks from AI-assisted ones across its catalog. The initiative gives buyers transparency about the creative process and puts pressure on other download and streaming platforms to follow suit.

music.title: Over 21 million copyrighted songs are circulating among AI developers, watchdog tool launched
music.excerpt: A new watchdog tool has identified over 21 million copyrighted songs being used in AI training datasets without license or consent. The tool, aimed at rights holders and music publishers, allows rightsholders to scan AI training corpora and flag unauthorized use of their catalog.

music.title: Tracktion Releases Waveform Free 14 With AI Assistant
music.excerpt: Tracktion has released Waveform Free 14, the no-cost tier of its DAW, which includes the new LLM-driven AI Assistant introduced in Waveform 14. The assistant can answer software questions, generate MIDI sequences, and analyze audio clips to suggest complementary parts, making advanced AI-assisted production tools available to bedroom producers at no cost.

music.title: Yamaha Creator Pass Adds Cubase, Ableton, And New $9.99 Starter Plan
music.excerpt: Yamaha's Creator Pass subscription service has expanded to include Cubase and Ableton Live alongside its existing AI-powered tools, and introduced a new $9.99 starter tier. The bundle approach signals a shift toward AI-integrated all-in-one subscription models for music creators, packaging composition tools, instruments, and AI features under one monthly fee.

music.title: Tidal isn't banning AI music, but it won't pay people who upload it
music.excerpt: Tidal's new AI music policy stops short of an outright ban but cuts off monetization for AI-generated tracks starting July 15, while still allowing them on the platform for non-paying listeners. The nuanced stance lets the platform remain a broad catalog while protecting human artists' share of the royalty pool.

music.title: YouTube's AI Training Argument Raises Concerns in the Indie Music Community
music.excerpt: Google's argument that YouTube's terms of service permit AI training on user-uploaded music has alarmed independent-music advocates, who contend the broad 2019 licensing language does not constitute informed consent for generative AI. 'These terms were not written with AI training in mind,' says the Music Artists Coalition.

movies.title: Panjaya and Shortical Partner on AI-Powered Dubbing and Localization for Microdrama Platform
movies.excerpt: Panjaya.ai and Shortical partnered to bring AI-powered localization across six languages, automating translation, voice-performance adaptation, timing and lip-sync while preserving the source's emotional register.

movies.title: Blending Animation and AI for Short Film 'Dear Upstairs Neighbors'
movies.excerpt: Pixar alum Connie He directed 'Dear Upstairs Neighbors,' a Tribeca-premiering short made with Google DeepMind's custom video-generation models alongside a 45-person crew of former Pixar collaborators, blending AI-generated visuals with traditional animation craft.

movies.title: Utopai and Huace Announce Fully AI-Generated 'Journey to the West' Animated Series
movies.excerpt: Utopai Studios and China's Huace Film & TV announced a co-production of a fully AI-generated animated series 'Journey to the West: The Lost Five Hundred Years,' using Utopai's cinematic storytelling AI system PAI, with a planned theatrical franchise.

movies.title: Mark Stern and Lloyd Braun Launch Intelligent Animation Studio with Proprietary AI Tools
movies.excerpt: Mark Stern and Lloyd Braun launched Intelligent Animation, a studio fusing traditional animation with proprietary AI tools, driven by the philosophy that new technologies should serve a human-led creative process — enabling faster, cheaper production without displacing artists.

movies.title: A24 / Google DeepMind Partnership Is Really About Workflow: What the $75M Deal Means
movies.excerpt: IndieWire's analysis of the A24/Google DeepMind $75M partnership focuses on workflow access — DeepMind gains insight into A24's production culture in exchange for building AI tools, making this a bet on shaping the future of indie filmmaking infrastructure.

movies.title: Adobe Acquires Topaz Labs to Expand AI Video Enhancement Tools
movies.excerpt: Adobe has agreed to acquire Topaz Labs, whose Emmy Award-winning AI models for video upscaling, noise reduction, and image enhancement will be integrated across Adobe's creative AI portfolio including Premiere Pro and After Effects for post-production pipelines.

movies.title: Adobe Acquires Topaz Labs: What It Means for Filmmakers
movies.excerpt: No Film School covers Adobe's Topaz Labs acquisition, explaining what it means for filmmakers: industry-leading AI upscaling and restoration built into Creative Cloud, lowering the barrier for archival footage enhancement and high-quality finishing.

movies.title: Neon Acquires Luca Guadagnino's AI-Themed Film 'Artificial' After Amazon MGM Drop
movies.excerpt: Neon acquired 'Artificial,' Luca Guadagnino's nearly completed film starring Andrew Garfield as Sam Altman, after Amazon MGM dropped it; the film's journey from prestige streaming to boutique distributor reflects how AI-themed content itself is navigating an unsettled industry.

movies.title: Is AI Hollywood's New Cosmetic Surgery? Studios Use It Quietly — and Don't Credit It
movies.excerpt: Studios are quietly using AI to smooth rough edges, alter dialogue, and polish visual effects in finished films while keeping the credits clean — one AI company is credited on only a fifth of the projects it works on, with nearly 80% of its work going unacknowledged including contributions to a 2026 box office hit.

movies.title: Adobe to Acquire Topaz Labs, Expanding AI Image and Video Tools
movies.excerpt: postPerspective covers Adobe's Topaz Labs acquisition from a post-production trade perspective, detailing how AI-driven upscaling, restoration, and noise-reduction tools will fold into professional finishing pipelines for film and episodic work.

movies.title: How 'Love Hurts' Embraced End-to-End Cloud Production with AI-Assisted Post
movies.excerpt: Universal Pictures used Love Hurts as a testbed for end-to-end cloud-based production and post with AWS, Company 3, and Pixelogic — centralizing AI-assisted dailies, VFX plate pulls, color, and mastering into a single cloud repository, a model likely to influence how major films are made.

games.title: Game Developers React to Unreal Engine 6 AI Integration: Resignation and Copyright Worry
games.excerpt: Aftermath gathers developer reactions to Epic embedding Claude, Gemini, and Codex as central to Unreal Engine 6 via MCP, finding widespread resignation and copyright worry rather than enthusiasm, especially among artists whose existing workflows are being sunset.

games.title: CDPR Co-CEO: Fully AI-Made Games Are 'Incoming' — But Should the Industry Follow?
games.excerpt: CD Projekt Red co-CEO Michal Nowakowski says fully AI-made games are 'incoming' after meeting a startup that can ship a complete game three weeks into prototyping, but questions whether that is really the path the industry should follow.

games.title: PlayStation Swaps 'PC' for 'AI' in Its Strategic Messaging — Patch Notes
games.excerpt: Game Developer's weekly Patch Notes column flags Sony/PlayStation's strategic pivot toward AI (swapping 'PC' for 'AI' in its messaging), alongside Rockstar's union legal setback and Final Fantasy tax incentives.

games.title: Generative AI Is a 'Plague,' Says Dragon Age Vet David Gaider — It's Not Ready for Prime Time
games.excerpt: Dragon Age narrative lead David Gaider calls generative AI a 'virulent plague' unfit for game development, arguing it is not ready for prime time and that executives pushing it onto creative teams are setting those teams up to fail.

games.title: As Sweeney Blasts Valve Over Steam AI Disclosures, Dev Releases Tool That Hides AI Games From Search
games.excerpt: As Epic CEO Tim Sweeney campaigns against Steam's mandatory AI content disclosures, a developer releases a browser plugin that does the opposite: making AI disclosures unmissable and blurring AI-assisted games out of Steam search results entirely.

games.title: Former Dragon Age Writer: AI in Gamedev Would Be 'Frustrating as Hell' — Who Trains the Next Generation?
games.excerpt: Former Dragon Age writer David Gaider warns that relying on generative AI to automate entry-level game dev tasks would be 'frustrating as hell' since AI cannot reliably iterate, and asks how the next generation of developers will be trained if all junior work is eliminated.

games.title: 'Finding the Fun' Remains Human Work for Two More Decades — But AI Could Spark a Gaming Renaissance
games.excerpt: Developer Rich Vogel argues that 'finding the fun' remains human creative work for at least two more decades, but contends AI could nonetheless spark a new gaming renaissance by enabling faster content generation and emergent gameplay never before feasible.

games.title: Tim Sweeney: Unreal Engine AI Tools Are an 'Accelerant for Real Creators,' Not a Slop Machine
games.excerpt: Epic CEO Tim Sweeney acknowledges that Unreal Engine's new AI integrations risk producing 'AI slop,' but argues they serve as an accelerant for genuine creators — indie and triple-A alike — who remain firmly in control of the final product.

games.title: Epic Loses Level Design Legend Sjoerd de Jong After 12 Years Over UE6's AI Pivot
games.excerpt: Unreal Engine evangelist and level design legend Sjoerd de Jong leaves Epic Games after 12 years, citing the industry's pivot toward AI-driven workflows and the deprecation of Blueprints as the signal that his era has come to a close.

books.title: Artificial intelligence already embedded in publishing in China, Beijing International Book Fair hears
books.excerpt: AI is now embedded in every stage of the editorial process in China, from subject selection to print production, the director of the Beijing International Book Fair organizing office told international journalists. A new Internet Publishing Joint Pavilion showcased AI-generated content tools and publishing workflow applications from NetEase, Tencent, and ByteDance.

books.title: Beijing International Book Fair Highlights Digital Publishing, Cross-Media Adaptations
books.excerpt: The 40th Beijing International Book Fair attracted 1,700 exhibitors from 82 countries with digital publishing and AI as hot topics; a dedicated exhibition hall hosted digital players such as NetEase, Tencent, and ByteDance-owned Fanqie Novel showcasing AI-generated content tools. Cross-media IP adaptation and AI-assisted publishing workflows were the dominant themes across forums.

books.title: Creative Satisfaction, In Person Print Book Sales, And Author Mindset With Mark Leslie Lefebvre
books.excerpt: Mark Leslie Lefebvre discusses managing divisiveness in the writing community around AI, the case for and against licensing your voice clone through ElevenLabs, and how to protect your creativity when machines never sleep. The episode also covers why print and in-person events are making a comeback for indie authors alongside AI narration adoption.

books.title: At the AUPresses Conference, Publishers Strive for Durability and Trust
books.excerpt: More than 470 university press professionals gathered in Seattle to discuss AI, scholarly rigor, and standing the test of time; sessions produced early drafts of AI standards for operations. The conference reflected how academic publishers are wrestling with AI adoption while maintaining the trust that distinguishes peer-reviewed scholarly publishing.

books.title: Around the Book World: Bookpact.ai Launches; ElevenLabs Produces First In-House AI Audiobook
books.excerpt: Dutch publishers launched Bookpact.ai, a new opt-in platform letting publishers manage title-by-title AI licensing, set compensation terms, and monitor how books are used by AI companies. The roundup also covers ElevenLabs' first in-house AI-produced audiobook, The Odyssey, narrated by an officially licensed AI voice of Michael Caine.

books.title: Mixed feelings: neurodiversity, authorship and AI
books.excerpt: Machine learning tools can support neurodiverse authors with their writing, but the piece interrogates whether they should—weighing accessibility benefits against concerns about what counts as authentic human authorship. The commentary examines the complex intersection of AI writing aids, disability, and literary identity.

books.title: Beijing Book Fair 2026: AI Tools Drive Children's Digital Publishing Innovation
books.excerpt: At BIBF 2026, the children's publishing segment showcased how digital publishing is now centered on audiobooks, e-learning platforms, and knowledge-based services, with AI tools supporting content creation and distribution. Publishers discussed AI-assisted editorial workflows for children's content as China's new reading-promotion regulations drive demand.
